ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON DEC-15-2009 IN DEPT. 206, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO SPECIALLY SET A TRIAL DATE GRANTED PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 36 (D). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 3/29/2010 @ 11:15 AM IN DEPT. 206. PLAINTIFF TO CONTACT DEPT. 502 FOR SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE DATES AND GIVE NOTICE. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. ALL DISCOVERY MOTIONS WILL BE NOTICED AND HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 206. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT BY 30 DAYS AFTER THE GRANTING OF PREFERENCE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES AFTER THE DUE DATE THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT IDENTIFIED PREVIOUSLY. ALL DEFENDANTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL RELY ON FOR PROPOSITION 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES SUPPORT, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 50 DAYS AFTER THE GRANTING OF PREFERENCE. IF DEFENDANTS LEARN OF NEW WITNESSES AND OR DOCUMENTS, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED PREVIOUSLY. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: KATHERINE FEINSTEIN; CLERK: E. BURA; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

ASBESTOS DISCOVERY, 220, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION TESTIMONY BY PERCIPIENT WITNESS DEBORAH ANN BRADY AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,955. NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: OFF CALENDAR. MATTER TO BE HEARD IN DEPARMENT 206 AT 9:30 AM. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR # 8600

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AND ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO ISSUES 1 (NEGLIGENCE), 2 (STRICT LIABILITY), 7 (LOSS OF CONSORTIUM) IS DENIED AND AS TO ISSUE 8 (PUNITIVE DAMAGES) IS GRANTED. MR. RIGG'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND THE DECLARATIONS OF STANLEY DAKE AND WILLIAM EWING CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. RIGG WAS EXPOSED TO DUST FROM AN ARC CHUTE COMPONENT OF DEFENDANT'S PRODUCT. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCTION ON THE ISSUE OF THE ASBESTOS CONTENT OF ARC CHUTES BECAUSE ITS GO 129 INTERROGATORY RESPONSES STATE THAT SOME OF ITS SWITCH GEARS MAY HAVE CONTAINED ASBESTOS COMPONENTS. DR HORNE'S DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. RIGG'S EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING HIS DISEASE. DEFENDANT'S ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO ISSUES 3 (FALSE REPRESENTATION), 4 (INTENTIONAL TORT), 5 (BREACH OF WARRANTY) AND 6 (PREMISES/CONTRACTOR LIABILITY) IS MOOT AS THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION HAVE BEEN DISMISSED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR # 8600

ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT EATON ELECTRICAL, INC. FKA CUTLER-HAMMER, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT GENERAL ELECTRICAL COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. DEFENDANT EATON ELECTRICAL INC. FKA CUTLER-HAMMER INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUES 1 (NEGLIGENCE), 2 (STRICT LIABILITY) AND 7 (LOSS OF CONSORTIUM). MR. RIGG'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND THE DECLARATIONS OF STANLEY DAKE AND WILLIAM EWING CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. RIGG WAS EXPOSED TO DUST FROM A CUTLER-HAMMER PRODUCT. DEFENDANT'S INTERROGATORY RESPONSES CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER THE 'ARC SHIELD/CHUTE' COMPONENTS OF DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTS CONTAINED ASBESTOS. DR HORNE'S DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. RIGG'S EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING HIS DISEASE. DEFENDANT'S ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO ISSUES 3 (INTENTIONAL TORT), 4 (FALSE REPRESENTATION), 5 (BREACH OF WARRANTY) AND 6 (PREMISES/CONTRACTOR LIABILITY) AND 8 (PUNITIVE DAMAGES) IS MOOT AS THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION HAVE BEEN DISMISSED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR # 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-25-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Paul J. Riehle, Esq. of SEDGWICK (415-781-7900), appearing for defendant / moving party GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY
Katherine Wang, Esq. of HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN (415-788-1588) for plaintiff / opposing party DALE RIGG et al / opposing party

RULING:
Argued and the Court modified it's tentative ruling and adopted as follows: Defendant General Electric Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication as to issues 1 (negligence), 2 (strict liability), 7 (loss of consortium) is denied and as to issue 8 (punitive damages) is granted. Mr. Rigg's deposition testimony and the declarations of Stanley Dake and William Ewing create a triable issue whether Mr. Rigg was exposed to dust from an arc chute component of defendant's product. Defendant failed to shift the burden of production on the issue of the asbestos content of arc chutes because its GO 129 interrogatory responses state that some of its switch gears may have contained asbestos components. Dr Horne's declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Rigg's exposure to asbestos was a substantial factor in causing his disease. Defendant's alternate motion for summary Adjudication as to issues 3 (false representation), 4 (intentional tort), 5 (breach of warranty) and 6 (premises/contractor liability) is moot as those causes of action have been dismissed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR # 8600 =(220/HEK)

Off Calendar Mar-25-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-25-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Darin A. Leviloff, Esq. of HOWARD ROME MARTIN & RIDLEY LLP (650-365-7715, X226), appearing for defendant / moving party EATON ELECTRICAL INC.
Katherine Wang, Esq. of HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN (415-788-1588) for plaintiff / opposing party DALE RIGG et al

RULING:
Argued and tentative ruling adopted. Defendant Eaton Electrical Inc. FKA CUTLER-HAMMER INC.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (negligence), 2 (strict liability) and 7 (loss of consortium). Mr. Rigg's deposition testimony and the declarations of Stanley Dake and William Ewing create a triable issue whether Mr. Rigg was exposed to dust from a Cutler-Hammer product. Defendant's interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether the 'arc shield/chute' components of defendant's products contained asbestos. Dr Horne's declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Rigg's exposure to asbestos was a substantial factor in causing his disease. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication as to issues 3 (intentional tort), 4 (False Representation), 5 (breach of warranty) and 6 (premises/contractor liability) and 8 (punitive damages) is moot as those causes of action have been dismissed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR # 8600 =(220/HEK)
